qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: API Request component SSRF guard is non-enforcing and still fetches localhost or internal targets
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets APIRequest SSRF via `validate_url_for_ssrf(url, warn_only=True)`, not the advisory's unauthenticated `build_public_tmp` flow-building path that accepts attacker-supplied graph data and reaches custom-component `exec()`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default auto-login grants unauthenticated superuser access and reaches code execution via /api/v1/validate/code
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding relies on the public `auto_login` token-minting path and then abuses `/api/v1/validate/code`; the advisory is a different unauthenticated route, `/api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow`, where attacker-supplied flow data reaches custom-component graph building.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings identifies the advisory's root cause: the unauthenticated `build_public_tmp` endpoint accepting attacker-controlled flow data and routing it into the public-flow build chain to `exec()` custom component code.
evidence: The advisory is scoped to `POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow` with attacker-controlled `data` flowing through `start_flow_build` -> `Graph.from_payload` -> `prepare_global_scope` -> `exec()`.

The `auto_login_validate_code_rce` finding instead uses `GET /api/v1/auto_login` to mint a superuser session and then `POST /api/v1/validate/code`; it does not involve `build_public_tmp`, public-flow building, or attacker-supplied flow graph data.

The `api_request_ssrf` finding instead targets `APIRequestComponent.make_api_request` and `validate_url_for_ssrf(url, warn_only=True)`, which is an SSRF boundary failure unrelated to the advisory's custom-component code-execution path.
